summary: Add protected Radar Task list, create, detail, edit, delete, and mock Run Scan UI on top of the existing API.
acceptance_criteria: A protected /radar-tasks list page displays persisted Radar Tasks with empty/loading/error-aware server-rendered states and links to create/detail flows.
A protected /radar-tasks/new page provides a Zod-aligned form for creating Radar Tasks using the existing local API or server action boundary.
A protected /radar-tasks/[id] detail page shows the task fields, recent run/opportunity context where available, and a mock Run Scan control using the existing /api/scans/run path.
A protected edit flow lets the local admin update task fields and a delete flow removes a task with an explicit confirmation UI.
The UI remains local-admin only, keeps mock provider as the default scan path, and does not add external provider keys, public sharing, ownership, or production deployment assumptions.

Focused automated tests cover form payload mapping/validation, list/detail view models, protected navigation assumptions where practical, and successful create/update/delete/run-scan request shapes without requiring a browser or network.
npm test, npm run build, and the final local release smoke pass.
notes: Derived from docs/11_development_plan.md M2 and docs/10_acceptance_criteria_and_test_plan.md flows 2 and 3.
systems_architect review is required because this adds client/server UI boundaries over the existing Radar Task API.
security_reviewer review is required because protected pages must cooperate with the local admin gate without weakening it.
non_goals: No new Radar Task API contract unless a narrow defect blocks the UI and is documented.
No production auth, multi-user ownership, real provider setup, paid service, public deployment, CI/CD, DNS, cron, email, billing, telemetry, or package dependency changes.
No Prisma schema migration or destructive database reset.
No changes to scoring, opportunity analysis, LLM, SERP provider behavior, or MVP Spec generation.
